claims.text: Differential Privacy (DP) has become a gold standard for making formal statements about data anonymization.

claims.text: Adoption of DP in real-world complex ML models remains limited despite some industry adoption.
claims.quote: while some adoption of DP has happened in industry, attempts to apply DP to real world complex ML models are still few and far between.

claims.text: Practical guidance on DP protection, privacy guarantees, and achieving good privacy-utility-computation trade-offs for ML models is currently limited.
claims.quote: The adoption of DP is hindered by limited practical guidance of what DP protection entails, what privacy guarantees to aim for, and the difficulty of achieving good privacy-utility-computation trade-offs for ML models.

claims.text: Tricks for tuning and maximizing performance of DP ML models, particularly for hyperparameter tuning, are scattered among papers or stored in the heads of practitioners.
claims.quote: Tricks for tuning and maximizing performance are scattered among papers or stored in the heads of practitioners, particularly with respect to the challenging task of hyperparameter tuning.

claims.text: The literature presents conflicting evidence on how and whether to apply architectural adjustments and which components are safe to use with DP.

claims.text: This survey paper proposes a set of specific best practices for stating DP privacy guarantees.
claims.quote: consistently and fully reporting privacy guarantees is critical, so we propose a set of specific best practices for stating guarantees.

claims.text: With sufficient computation and a sufficiently large training set or supplemental non-private data, both good accuracy (almost as good as a non-private model) and good privacy can often be achievable simultaneously.
claims.quote: With sufficient computation and a sufficiently large training set or supplemental nonprivate data, both good accuracy (that is, almost as good as a non-private model) and good privacy can often be achievable.

claims.text: There are advantages to training with even a weak but still finite formal DP guarantee, even when computation and dataset size are limited.
claims.quote: even when computation and dataset size are limited, there are advantages to training with even a weak (but still finite) formal DP guarantee.

claims.text: This survey provides a step-by-step guide for choosing an appropriate privacy definition and approach, implementing DP training, potentially updating the model architecture, and tuning hyperparameters.

claims.text: Traditional static machine learning models are insufficient for addressing rapidly shifting fraud threats in financial environments.
claims.quote: Traditional static models often fall short in addressing these rapidly shifting threats, necessitating the adoption of adaptive machine learning techniques.

claims.text: Adaptive machine learning models use reinforcement learning, online learning, and deep learning algorithms to maintain effectiveness in fraud detection.
claims.quote: These models employ advanced algorithms, such as reinforcement learning, online learning, and deep learning, to maintain their effectiveness in detecting and preventing fraud.

claims.text: Reinforcement learning algorithms improve fraud detection by optimizing strategies through feedback from their actions.
claims.quote: Reinforcement learning algorithms optimize detection strategies by receiving feedback from their actions, continually improving their decision-making processes.

claims.text: Online learning algorithms update fraud detection models incrementally as new transaction data becomes available.
claims.quote: Online learning algorithms update models incrementally as new transaction data becomes available, ensuring that the models remain current and responsive.

claims.text: Neural networks and ensemble learning enable adaptive models to analyze complex datasets and detect fraudulent activities with high precision.
claims.quote: By leveraging technologies such as neural networks and ensemble learning, these models can analyze complex datasets, identify subtle anomalies, and detect fraudulent activities with high precision.

claims.text: Real-time data processing capabilities in adaptive models enable immediate detection and response to suspicious transactions, reducing financial losses.
claims.quote: Real-time data processing capabilities enable immediate detection and response to suspicious transactions, significantly reducing the risk of financial losses.

claims.text: Adaptive machine learning models incorporate anomaly detection techniques to identify deviations from normal transaction behavior and recognize previously unseen fraud patterns.
claims.quote: Adaptive models also incorporate anomaly detection techniques to identify deviations from normal transaction behavior. By constantly learning from the latest data, these models can recognize previously unseen fraud patterns.

claims.text: Integration of explainable AI (XAI) techniques into adaptive models ensures transparency and interpretability of decision-making processes, supporting regulatory compliance.
claims.quote: the integration of explainable AI (XAI) techniques ensures that the decision-making processes of these models are transparent and interpretable, fostering trust and compliance with regulatory requirements.

claims.text: Implementing adaptive machine learning models for real-time fraud prevention involves challenges including data quality, computational efficiency, and model interpretability.
claims.quote: Implementing adaptive machine learning models for real-time fraud prevention involves addressing challenges such as data quality, computational efficiency, and model interpretability.

claims.text: Financial institutions must invest in robust computational infrastructure to support real-time processing of adaptive fraud detection models.
claims.quote: Financial institutions must ensure the availability of high-quality data and invest in robust computational infrastructure to support real-time processing.

claims.text: GRiT is a Generative Region-to-Text transformer designed for object understanding.
claims.quote: This paper presents a Generative RegIon-to-Text transformer, GRiT, for object understanding.

claims.text: GRiT formulates object understanding as region-text pairs, where regions locate objects and text describes objects.
claims.quote: The spirit of GRiT is to formulate object understanding as pairs, where region locates objects and text describes objects.

claims.text: In object detection, the text component of GRiT denotes class names, while in dense captioning it refers to descriptive sentences.
claims.quote: the text in object detection denotes class names while that in dense captioning refers to descriptive sentences.

claims.text: GRiT consists of three components: a visual encoder to extract image features, a foreground object extractor to localize objects, and a text decoder to generate open-set object descriptions.
claims.quote: GRiT consists of a visual encoder to extract image features, a foreground object extractor to localize objects, and a text decoder to generate open-set object descriptions.

claims.text: GRiT uses the same model architecture for both object detection and dense captioning tasks.
claims.quote: With the same model architecture, GRiT can understand objects via not only simple nouns, but also rich descriptive sentences including object attributes or actions.

claims.text: GRiT can describe objects using both simple nouns and rich descriptive sentences that include object attributes or actions.
claims.quote: GRiT can understand objects via not only simple nouns, but also rich descriptive sentences including object attributes or actions.

claims.text: GRiT achieves 60.4 AP on the COCO 2017 test-dev benchmark for object detection.
claims.quote: GRiT achieves 60.4 AP on COCO 2017 test-dev for object detection

claims.text: GRiT achieves 15.5 mAP on the Visual Genome dataset for dense captioning.
claims.quote: 15.5 mAP on Visual Genome for dense captioning.

claims.text: The code for GRiT is publicly available on GitHub at https://github.com/JialianW/GRiT.
claims.quote: Code is available at https://github.com/JialianW/GRiT

claims.text: Flamingo is a family of Visual Language Models (VLM) designed for rapid adaptation to novel tasks using only a handful of annotated examples.
claims.quote: We introduce Flamingo, a family of Visual Language Models (VLM) with this ability.

claims.text: Flamingo proposes architectural innovations to bridge powerful pretrained vision-only and language-only models.
claims.quote: bridge powerful pretrained vision-only and language-only models

claims.text: Flamingo is designed to handle sequences of arbitrarily interleaved visual and textual data.
claims.quote: handle sequences of arbitrarily interleaved visual and textual data

claims.text: Flamingo models can seamlessly ingest both images and videos as inputs.
claims.quote: seamlessly ingest images or videos as inputs

claims.text: Flamingo models are trained on large-scale multimodal web corpora containing arbitrarily interleaved text and images.
claims.quote: Flamingo models can be trained on large-scale multimodal web corpora containing arbitrarily interleaved text and images

claims.text: Training on large-scale multimodal web corpora is key to endowing Flamingo models with in-context few-shot learning capabilities.
claims.quote: which is key to endow them with in-context few-shot learning capabilities

claims.text: A single Flamingo model can achieve a new state of the art with few-shot learning across tasks ranging from open-ended to close-ended, simply by prompting the model with task-specific examples.
claims.quote: a single Flamingo model can achieve a new state of the art with few-shot learning, simply by prompting the model with task-specific examples

claims.text: On numerous benchmarks, Flamingo outperforms models that have been fine-tuned on thousands of times more task-specific data.

claims.text: Flamingo was evaluated on open-ended tasks such as visual question-answering and captioning, as well as close-ended tasks such as multiple-choice visual question-answering.
claims.quote: These include open-ended tasks such as visual question-answering...captioning tasks...and close-ended tasks such as multiple-choice visual question-answering

claims.text: Zero123++ is an image-conditioned diffusion model designed to generate 3D-consistent multi-view images from a single input view.

claims.text: The authors developed conditioning and training schemes to minimize the effort required to finetune from off-the-shelf image diffusion models such as Stable Diffusion.

claims.text: Zero123++ leverages pretrained 2D generative priors to generate multi-view images.

claims.text: A building digital twin (BDT) can maintain an up-to-date digital model reflecting physical world conditions.

claims.text: Recent studies on building digital twins employed the Internet of Things to sense physical-world conditions.

claims.text: The proposed BDT scheme takes camera videos as input and uses building information modeling (BIM) to address dimension, coordinate system, and object inconsistencies between BIM and camera videos.

claims.text: The proposed BDT scheme detects objects' locations and rotations jointly using a 2-D object detection network and a 3-D object estimation network.
claims.quote: the proposed BDT scheme detects objects' locations and rotations jointly using a 2-D object detection network and a 3-D object estimation network

claims.text: The proposed scheme uses theorems and lemmas to compute 3-D locations in the building coordinate system (BCS) using detected 2-D locations.
claims.quote: theorem and lemmas are presented to compute the 3-D locations in BCS using detected 2-D locations

claims.text: Both cold-start object matching and run-time object matching schemes are proposed to address object inconsistency between camera videos and BIM.
claims.quote: both cold-start object matching and run-time object matching schemes are proposed to address the object inconsistency between camera videos and BIM

claims.text: In the manual calibration scenario with distortions preserved, the proposed BDT scheme achieved an average location error of 0.181 meters.
claims.quote: average location errors of 0.181 m with distortions preserved and 0.165 m with distortions removed in the manual calibration scenario

claims.text: In the manual calibration scenario with distortions removed, the proposed BDT scheme achieved an average location error of 0.165 meters.
claims.quote: 0.165 m with distortions removed in the manual calibration scenario

claims.text: In the automatic calibration scenario with distortions preserved, the proposed BDT scheme achieved an average location error of 0.166 meters.
claims.quote: 0.166 m with distortions preserved, and 0.195 m with distortions removed in automatic calibration scenario

claims.text: In the automatic calibration scenario with distortions removed, the proposed BDT scheme achieved an average location error of 0.195 meters.
claims.quote: 0.195 m with distortions removed in automatic calibration scenario

claims.text: This study is the first to explore a building digital twin scheme on top of BIM using computer vision.
claims.quote: This study is the first to explore a BDT scheme on top of BIM using CV

claims.text: Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) are vulnerable to adversarial attacks in computer vision tasks.
claims.quote: Despite the impressive achievements of Deep Neural Networks (DNNs) in computer vision, their vulnerability to adversarial attacks remains a critical concern.

claims.text: Incorporating sophisticated perturbations into input images can cause catastrophic degradation in DNNs' performance.
claims.quote: Extensive research has demonstrated that incorporating sophisticated perturbations into input images can lead to a catastrophic degradation in DNNs' performance.

claims.text: Adversarial attacks against DNNs exist not only in the digital space but also in the physical world.
claims.quote: This perplexing phenomenon not only exists in the digital space but also in the physical world.

claims.text: The paper identifies four general steps for launching physical adversarial attacks.
claims.quote: we distill four general steps for launching physical adversarial attacks.

claims.text: The paper introduces a new term, 'adversarial medium,' to denote physical artifacts that carry adversarial perturbations in the physical world.
claims.quote: we introduce a new term: adversarial medium.

claims.text: Adversarial mediums influence each of the four steps involved in launching physical adversarial attacks.
claims.quote: These artifacts influence each step.

claims.text: The paper proposes an evaluation metric called hiPAA for systematically evaluating the performance of physical adversarial attacks.
claims.quote: Our proposed evaluation metric, hiPAA, comprises six perspectives: Effectiveness, Stealthiness, Robustness, Practicability, Aesthetics, and Economics.

claims.text: The hiPAA evaluation metric comprises six perspectives: Effectiveness, Stealthiness, Robustness, Practicability, Aesthetics, and Economics.
claims.quote: Our proposed evaluation metric, hiPAA, comprises six perspectives: Effectiveness, Stealthiness, Robustness, Practicability, Aesthetics, and Economics.

claims.text: The paper uses the adversarial medium as the primary basis for systematically evaluating physical adversarial attacks for the first time.
claims.quote: we take the first step to systematically evaluate the performance of physical adversarial attacks, taking the adversarial medium as a first attempt.

claims.text: The paper provides comparative results across task categories for physical adversarial attacks.
claims.quote: We also provide comparative results across task categories, together with insightful observations and suggestions for future research directions.

claims.text: Recommender systems have seen significant advancements due to the influence of deep learning and graph neural networks.
claims.quote: Recommender systems have seen significant advancements with the influence of deep learning and graph neural networks, particularly in capturing complex user-item relationships.

claims.text: Graph-based recommenders heavily depend on ID-based data and potentially disregard valuable textual information associated with users and items.
claims.quote: these graph-based recommenders heavily depend on ID-based data, potentially disregarding valuable textual information associated with users and items, resulting in less informative learned representations.

claims.text: The use of implicit feedback data introduces potential noise and bias, which poses challenges for effective user preference learning.

claims.text: Integrating large language models into traditional ID-based recommenders faces challenges including scalability issues, limitations in text-only reliance, and prompt input constraints.

claims.text: The authors propose a model-agnostic framework called RLMRec that enhances existing recommenders with LLM-empowered representation learning.
claims.quote: we propose a model-agnostic framework RLMRec that aims to enhance existing recommenders with LLM-empowered representation learning.

claims.text: RLMRec incorporates auxiliary textual signals and employs LLMs for user and item profiling.
claims.quote: RLMRec incorporates auxiliary textual signals, employs LLMs for user/item profiling

claims.text: RLMRec aligns the semantic space of LLMs with collaborative relational signals through cross-view alignment.
claims.quote: aligns the semantic space of LLMs with collaborative relational signals through cross-view alignment.

claims.text: The theoretical foundation for incorporating textual signals in RLMRec is demonstrated through mutual information maximization, which improves representation quality.
claims.quote: This work further demonstrates the theoretical foundation of incorporating textual signals through mutual information maximization, which improves the quality of representations.

claims.text: The evaluation of RLMRec integrates it with state-of-the-art recommender models and analyzes its efficiency and robustness to noise data.
claims.quote: Our evaluation integrates RLMRec with state-of-the-art recommender models, while also analyzing its efficiency and robustness to noise data.

claims.text: The implementation code for RLMRec is publicly available on GitHub at https://github.com/HKUDS/RLMRec.

claims.text: DiffuScene is a system for indoor 3D scene synthesis based on a scene configuration denoising diffusion model.

claims.text: Time series data collected by multiple spatially distributed sensors have been widely used in different research fields, including geo-tagged temperature data, air pollutant monitoring data, and traffic data.
claims.quote: time series data collected by multiple and spatially distributed sensors have been widely used in different research fields. Examples of such data include geo-tagged temperature data collected by temperature sensors, air pollutant monitoring data, and traffic data collected by road traffic sensors.

claims.text: Sensor-collected data inevitably includes missing data due to sensor failure, communication errors, and storage loss.
claims.quote: Due to sensor failure, communication errors and storage loss, etc., data collected by sensors inevitably includes missing data.

claims.quote: models commonly used in the analysis of such large-scale data often rely on complete data sets.

claims.text: The paper proposes a model called SA-GAIN (Self-Attention Generative Adversarial Imputation Net) for imputing missing traffic flow data, which combines a self-attention mechanism, an auto-encoder, and a generative adversarial network.
claims.quote: This paper proposes a model for the imputation of missing data of traffic flow, which combines a self-attention mechanism, an auto-encoder, and a generative adversarial network, into a self-attention generative adversarial imputation net (SA-GAIN).

claims.text: The self-attention mechanism in SA-GAIN helps the model effectively capture correlations between spatially-distributed sensors at different time points.
claims.quote: The introduction of the self-attention mechanism can help the proposed model to effectively capture correlations between spatially-distributed sensors at different time points.

claims.text: SA-GAIN uses adversarial training through two neural networks called generators and discriminators to generate imputed data close to the real data.
claims.quote: Adversarial training through two neural networks, called generators and discriminators, allows the proposed model to generate imputed data close to the real data.

claims.text: SA-GAIN shows the best performance in imputing missing data compared to different imputation models.
claims.quote: In comparison with different imputation models, the proposed model shows the best performance in imputing missing data.

claims.text: Position Interpolation extends the context window sizes of RoPE-based pretrained LLMs such as LLaMA models to up to 32768 tokens.
claims.quote: extends the context window sizes of RoPE-based pretrained LLMs such as LLaMA models to up to 32768

claims.text: Position Interpolation requires minimal fine-tuning, achieving context window extension within 1000 steps.
claims.quote: with minimal fine-tuning (within 1000 steps)

claims.quote: demonstrating strong empirical results on various tasks that require long context, including passkey retrieval, language modeling, and long document summarization

claims.text: Position Interpolation was evaluated on LLaMA models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters.
claims.quote: from LLaMA 7B to 65B

claims.text: Models extended via Position Interpolation preserve quality relatively well on tasks within the original context window size.
claims.quote: the extended model by Position Interpolation preserve quality relatively well on tasks within its original context window

claims.text: Position Interpolation works by linearly down-scaling input position indices to match the original context window size, rather than extrapolating beyond the trained context length.
claims.quote: Position Interpolation linearly down-scales the input position indices to match the original context window size, rather than extrapolating beyond the trained context length

claims.text: Extrapolating beyond the trained context length may lead to catastrophically high attention scores that completely ruin the self-attention mechanism.
claims.quote: extrapolating beyond the trained context length which may lead to catastrophically high attention scores that completely ruin the self-attention mechanism

claims.text: The theoretical upper bound of interpolation error is at least approximately 600 times smaller than that of extrapolation.
claims.quote: the upper bound of interpolation is at least ~600× smaller than that of extrapolation

claims.text: Models extended via Position Interpolation retain their original architecture and can reuse most pre-existing optimization and infrastructure.
claims.quote: Models extended via Position Interpolation retain its original architecture and can reuse most pre-existing optimization and infrastructure

claims.text: Iter-RetGen is a method that synergizes retrieval and generation in an iterative manner.
claims.quote: strong performance can be achieved by a method we call Iter-RetGen, which synergizes retrieval and generation in an iterative manner

claims.text: In Iter-RetGen, a model output provides an informative context for retrieving more relevant knowledge, which in turn helps generate a better output in the next iteration.

claims.text: Iter-RetGen processes all retrieved knowledge as a whole, unlike methods that interleave retrieval with generation when producing an output.

claims.quote: which is based on the blackbox commercial GPT-4 that has 50x (estimated) model size

claims.text: LaVin-DiT is designed to tackle over 20 computer vision tasks in a generative framework.
claims.quote: a scalable and unified foundation model designed to tackle over 20 computer vision tasks in a generative framework

claims.text: Existing large vision models directly adapted from NLP architectures rely on autoregressive techniques that are less efficient and disrupt spatial relationships essential for vision data.
claims.quote: existing large vision models directly adapted from natural language processing architectures, which rely on less efficient autoregressive techniques and disrupt spatial relationships essential for vision data

claims.text: LaVin-DiT uses a joint diffusion transformer that progressively produces vision outputs for generative modeling.

claims.text: LaVin-DiT implements in-context learning for unified multitask training, where input-target pairs serve as task context to guide the diffusion transformer.

claims.text: During inference, LaVin-DiT uses a task-specific context set and test data as queries to generalize across tasks without fine-tuning.

claims.text: LaVin-DiT is scaled from 0.1B to 3.4B parameters.
claims.quote: the model is scaled from 0.1B to 3.4B parameters

claims.text: LaVin-DiT demonstrates state-of-the-art performance across diverse vision tasks.
claims.quote: demonstrating substantial scalability and state-of-the-art performance across diverse vision tasks

claims.text: The proposed watermarking methodology plants watermarks in text from an autoregressive language model that are robust to perturbations without changing the distribution over text up to a certain maximum generation budget.

claims.text: Watermarked text is generated by mapping a sequence of random numbers, computed using a randomized watermark key, to a sample from the language model.
claims.quote: We generate watermarked text by mapping a sequence of random numbers -- which we compute using a randomized watermark key -- to a sample from the language model

claims.text: Detection of watermarked text requires knowledge of the watermark key, which is used to align the text to the random number sequence.
claims.quote: To detect watermarked text, any party who knows the key can align the text to the random number sequence

claims.text: The watermark methodology is instantiated with two sampling schemes: inverse transform sampling and exponential minimum sampling.
claims.quote: We instantiate our watermark methodology with two sampling schemes: inverse transform sampling and exponential minimum sampling

claims.text: The watermarks were applied to and experimentally validated on three language models: OPT-1.3B, LLaMA-7B, and Alpaca-7B.
claims.quote: We apply these watermarks to three language models -- OPT-1.3B, LLaMA-7B and Alpaca-7B -- to experimentally validate their statistical power and robustness to various paraphrasing attacks

claims.text: For OPT-1.3B and LLaMA-7B models, watermarked text can be reliably detected (p ≤ 0.01) from as few as 35 tokens even after corrupting between 40–50% of the tokens via random edits including substitutions, insertions, or deletions.
claims.quote: for both the OPT-1.3B and LLaMA-7B models, we find we can reliably detect watermarked text ($p \leq 0.01$) from $35$ tokens even after corrupting between $40$-$50\%$ of the tokens via random edits (i.e., substitutions, insertions or deletions)

claims.text: For the Alpaca-7B model, approximately 25% of responses are detectable with p ≤ 0.01, with the median response length being around 100 tokens.
claims.quote: around $25\%$ of the responses -- whose median length is around $100$ tokens -- are detectable with $p \leq 0.01$

claims.text: Watermark detection is more difficult for Alpaca-7B responses due to the lower entropy of those responses.
claims.quote: Due to the lower entropy of the responses, detection is more difficult

claims.text: The watermark applied to Alpaca-7B is less robust to certain automated paraphrasing attacks compared to the other models.
claims.quote: the watermark is also less robust to certain automated paraphrasing attacks we implement

claims.text: Sophia is a second-order optimizer that uses a lightweight estimate of the diagonal Hessian as a preconditioner.
claims.quote: a simple scalable second-order optimizer that uses a light-weight estimate of the diagonal Hessian as the pre-conditioner

claims.text: Sophia's update rule consists of the moving average of gradients divided by the moving average of the estimated Hessian, followed by element-wise clipping.
claims.quote: The update is the moving average of the gradients divided by the moving average of the estimated Hessian, followed by element-wise clipping

claims.text: Element-wise clipping in Sophia controls the worst-case update size and mitigates the negative impact of non-convexity and rapid Hessian changes along the training trajectory.
claims.quote: The clipping controls the worst-case update size and tames the negative impact of non-convexity and rapid change of Hessian along the trajectory

claims.text: Sophia estimates the diagonal Hessian only every few iterations, resulting in negligible average per-step time and memory overhead.

claims.text: Sophia achieves the same perplexity as Adam with 50% fewer steps on GPT models ranging from 125M to 1.5B parameters.
claims.quote: achieving the same perplexity with 50% fewer steps, less total compute, and reduced wall-clock time

claims.text: Sophia was evaluated on language modeling with GPT models of sizes ranging from 125M to 1.5B parameters.
claims.quote: On language modeling with GPT models of sizes ranging from 125M to 1.5B

claims.text: Theoretically, Sophia adapts to heterogeneous curvatures across different parameter dimensions in a simplified setting.
claims.quote: Sophia, in a much simplified setting, adapts to the heterogeneous curvatures in different parameter dimensions

claims.text: Sophia's theoretical runtime bound does not depend on the condition number of the loss function.
claims.quote: has a run-time bound that does not depend on the condition number of the loss

claims.text: Lagrangian turbulence is relevant to problems of dispersion and mixing in engineering, biofluids, the atmosphere, oceans, and astrophysics.

claims.text: No existing models prior to this work are capable of faithfully reproducing both statistical and topological properties of particle trajectories in turbulence, despite 30 years of research effort.
claims.quote: Despite exceptional theoretical, numerical and experimental efforts conducted over the past 30 years, no existing models are capable of faithfully reproducing statistical and topological properties exhibited by particle trajectories in turbulence.

claims.text: The authors propose a machine learning approach based on a diffusion model to generate single-particle trajectories in three-dimensional turbulence at high Reynolds numbers.

claims.text: The diffusion model bypasses the need for direct numerical simulations or experiments to obtain reliable Lagrangian turbulence data.
claims.quote: thereby bypassing the need for direct numerical simulations or experiments to obtain reliable Lagrangian data

claims.text: The proposed model reproduces most statistical benchmarks across time scales, including the fat-tail distribution for velocity increments, the anomalous power law, and increased intermittency around the dissipative scale.

claims.text: The model shows slight deviations from real turbulence statistics below the dissipative scale, particularly in acceleration and flatness statistics.
claims.quote: Slight deviations are observed below the dissipative scale, particularly in the acceleration and flatness statistics.

claims.text: The model exhibits generalizability to extreme events, generating events of higher intensity and rarity than those seen during training while still matching realistic statistics.
claims.quote: the model exhibits strong generalizability for extreme events, producing events of higher intensity and rarity that still match the realistic statistics.

claims.text: The diffusion model can generate synthetic high-quality Lagrangian turbulence datasets suitable for pretraining downstream applications.
claims.quote: This paves the way for producing synthetic high-quality datasets for pretraining various downstream applications of Lagrangian turbulence.

claims.text: The generated Lagrangian turbulence trajectories have statistical properties consistent with those of the training set.

claims.text: The diffusion model can generalize to rare, intense turbulence events that were not seen during training.
claims.quote: even generalize to rare, intense events unseen during training

claims.text: Fog computing has emerged as a computing paradigm designed to support time-sensitive and computationally intensive applications for resource-restricted IoT devices.

claims.text: Offloading transfers resource-intensive tasks from resource-limited end devices to a resource-rich fog or cloud layer to reduce end-to-end latency and enhance system performance.

claims.text: Current computer vision systems use an image signal processing (ISP) unit to convert high resolution raw images captured by image sensors to visually pleasing RGB images.

claims.text: The original trilogy problem (safety constraint satisfaction, reward maximization, and behavior regularization) can be converted to a feasibility-dependent objective of maximizing reward within the feasible region while minimizing safety risks in the infeasible region.

claims.text: Knowledge Distillation has been extensively studied as a means to enhance the performance of smaller models in Convolutional Neural Networks.
claims.quote: Knowledge Distillation (KD) has been extensively studied as a means to enhance the performance of smaller models in Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs).

claims.text: Feature-based Knowledge Distillation methods designed for CNNs cannot be directly applied to Vision Transformers due to significant structural differences.
claims.quote: while logit-based KD has been applied to ViT, other feature-based KD methods for CNNs cannot be directly implemented due to the significant structure gap.

claims.text: Both shallow and deep layers in Vision Transformers are equally important for distillation and require distinct distillation strategies.
claims.quote: Our findings reveal that both shallow and deep layers in ViT are equally important for distillation and require distinct distillation strategies.

claims.text: The proposed ViTKD method mimics the shallow layers and generates the deep layer of the teacher network.
claims.quote: we propose our feature-based method ViTKD, which mimics the shallow layers and generates the deep layer in the teacher.

claims.text: ViTKD achieves a performance boost of 1.64% for DeiT-Tiny on ImageNet-1K.
claims.quote: On ImageNet-1K, we achieve performance boosts of 1.64% for DeiT-Tiny

claims.text: ViTKD achieves a performance boost of 1.40% for DeiT-Small on ImageNet-1K.
claims.quote: 1.40% for DeiT-Small

claims.text: ViTKD achieves a performance boost of 1.70% for DeiT-Base on ImageNet-1K.
claims.quote: and 1.70% for DeiT-Base

claims.text: ViTKD and logit-based Knowledge Distillation are complementary and can be applied together to further enhance student model performance.
claims.quote: ViTKD and logit-based KD are complementary and can be applied together directly, further enhancing the student's performance.

claims.text: When combining ViTKD with logit-based KD, DeiT-Tiny, DeiT-Small, and DeiT-Base achieve accuracies of 77.78%, 83.59%, and 85.41% respectively on ImageNet-1K.
claims.quote: DeiT-T, S, and B achieve accuracies of 77.78%, 83.59%, and 85.41%, respectively, using this combined approach.

claims.text: ViTKD demonstrates superiority on downstream tasks beyond ImageNet-1K classification.
claims.quote: Downstream tasks also demonstrate the superiority of ViTKD.

claims.text: Offline reinforcement learning aims to learn an optimal policy using a previously collected static dataset.
